channel.title: The Olsen Tribune
channel.description: Daily business intelligence — markets, technology, healthcare, politics, and analysis.

item.title: Bank of England Deputy Breaks Protocol With Stark Warning on Equity Valuations
item.description: A rare public intervention from Threadneedle Street suggests global stock markets have drifted into territory the central bank considers unsustainable.

item.title: Meta's Ten Per Cent Cull Forces Investors to Confront the True Cost of the AI Build-Out
item.description: The social media group's largest workforce reduction since 2023 lays bare the trade-offs facing hyperscalers as artificial intelligence capital spending balloons.

item.title: Google's $40 Billion Anthropic Bet Redraws the Map of the AI Compute Wars
item.description: The search giant's deepening entanglement with a rival-turned-partner exposes a new reality: in artificial intelligence, capital and silicon have become indistinguishable.

item.title: After Cook: Apple Prepares for Its First Real Test of the Post-Jobs Era
item.description: John Ternus inherits a company at peak profitability but facing a generational reckoning over artificial intelligence, hardware shortages, and the limits of services-led growth.

item.title: The NHS Workforce Paradox: Trained, Qualified, and Unable to Find a Job
item.description: A growing cohort of newly minted midwives, nurses and physiotherapists say they are being shut out of the very system that funded their education.

item.title: Gene Therapy's Quiet Revolution Forces a Reckoning on Drug Pricing
item.description: As one-time treatments restore sight and rewrite prognoses, health systems must confront a payment model designed for chronic, not curative, medicine.

item.title: War Powers Clock Forces Congress Into a Reckoning Over Iran
item.description: As the statutory 60-day window closes on the administration's military engagement with Tehran, lawmakers face a vote many would rather avoid.

item.title: Justice Department Closes Powell Inquiry, Clearing Runway for Warsh
item.description: The decision to abandon a politically charged probe into the outgoing Fed chair removes a complication for the administration's nominee to lead the central bank.

item.title: BYD's Calculated Bet: Building a Global Empire Without the American Consumer
item.description: As fuel prices climb and Western markets close ranks, the Shenzhen-based automaker is rewriting the playbook for what a global car company looks like in the post-tariff era.

item.title: Threadneedle Street Breaks Protocol: Why a Rare Market Warning Demands Attention
item.description: When a Bank of England deputy governor publicly questions equity valuations, investors should consider what such an unusual departure from convention might signal about underlying fragility.

item.title: Bank of England Deputy Breaks Convention With Stark Warning on Equity Valuations
item.description: A rare public caution from Threadneedle Street rattles traders already grappling with geopolitical shocks and a fragile inflation picture.

item.title: Jet Fuel Shock Forces Airlines Into Capacity Retreat as Iran Conflict Reshapes Travel Economics
item.description: Lufthansa's decision to strip tens of thousands of flights from its summer schedule marks the clearest sign yet that carriers are bracing for a prolonged energy squeeze.

item.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Signals the Dawn of the AI Super App Era
item.description: With its latest model release, the Sam Altman-led company edges closer to a unified consumer platform that could reshape how users interact with software entirely.

item.title: Silicon Valley's Great Rebalancing: Meta and Microsoft Usher In a New Workforce Doctrine
item.description: Fresh rounds of cuts and buyouts at two of the industry's largest employers reveal a structural shift in how tech giants calibrate headcount against the productivity gains of artificial intelligence.

item.title: The NHS Workforce Paradox: Trained, Qualified, and Unemployed
item.description: Thousands of newly qualified midwives, nurses and physiotherapists are entering a hiring freeze even as waiting lists swell past record levels, exposing a fundamental mismatch between Britain's healthcare ambitions and its fiscal reality.

item.title: Gene Therapy's Quiet Inflection Point Arrives at the Bedside
item.description: A six-year-old's restored vision at Great Ormond Street is more than a human-interest story — it signals that one-shot genetic medicines are moving from experimental curiosity to reimbursed standard of care, with profound implications for pharmaceutical economics.

item.title: Johnson's Third Attempt to Salvage Surveillance Law Exposes GOP Fractures
item.description: With a April 30 sunset looming, the Speaker is racing to corral a caucus increasingly skeptical of the intelligence community's core authorities.

item.title: EU Unlocks $106 Billion for Kyiv as Orban Blinks, But Unity Remains Brittle
item.description: The bloc's largest single financial commitment to Ukraine since the war began papers over deep divisions that could resurface within months.

item.title: The Fuel-Price Feedback Loop: How a Distant War Is Rewriting Western Balance Sheets
item.description: From cancelled transatlantic flights to sticky inflation prints, the conflict in the Middle East is quietly imposing a tax on households and corporates alike.

item.title: The AI Labour Reckoning Arrives at Both Ends of the Career Ladder
item.description: Meta's latest cull and a warning from a former prime minister point to the same uncomfortable truth: the technology is eating jobs faster than it is creating them.
